text: When designing a REST API, clarity and consistency are essential. Start by naming your endpoints with nouns, such as `/users` or `/orders`, and use HTTP methods like GET, POST, PUT, and DELETE to perform actions. Always return appropriate status codes: 200 for success, 201 for creation, 400 for bad requests, and 404 for not found. Good documentation is crucial - it should explain available endpoints, required parameters, and expected responses. Finally, implement versioning in your API, for example `/v1/users`, so that changes don't break existing applications. Following these practices makes your API easier to use and maintain.
